pi_items.source: World Bank Open Data portal structure - verified URLs are valid and contain development indicator data
All three countries are Sub-Saharan African nations with comprehensive World Bank data available
Task setup and verifier.py explicitly check for this folder name in Firefox bookmarks
export_result.sh and verifier.py both check for file at this exact path
World Bank data shows Nigeria GDP: 2022=477.4B, 2023~463B USD. Both values fall within [250, 650] range.
World Bank data shows Nigeria GDP per capita: 2022=$2,139, 2023=$1,596.64 USD. Both within [1000, 3500] range.
World Bank data shows Nigeria life expectancy: 2022=54.079 years, 2023=54.462 years. Both within [48, 60] range.
World Bank data shows Nigeria population: 2022=223.15M, 2023=227.88M. Both within [190, 250] range.
World Bank data shows Nigeria electricity access: 2022=60.50%, 2023=61.2%. Both within [45, 80] range.
World Bank data shows Kenya GDP: 2022=114.45B, 2023=108.04B USD. Both within [70, 160] range.
World Bank data shows Kenya GDP per capita: 2022=$2,110 USD. Within [1500, 3000] range.
World Bank data shows Kenya life expectancy: 2022=63.55 years, 2023=63.65 years. Both within [55, 70] range.
World Bank data shows Kenya population: 2022=54.25M, 2023=55.34M. Both within [45, 65] range.
World Bank data shows Kenya electricity access: 2022=76%, 2023=76.2%. Both within [60, 90] range.
World Bank data shows South Africa GDP: 2022=406.92B USD. Within [300, 500] range.
World Bank data shows South Africa GDP per capita: 2022=$6,523.41, 2023=$6,022.54 USD. Both within [5000, 8000] range.
World Bank data shows South Africa life expectancy: 2022=65.45 years, 2023=66.14 years. Both within [58, 70] range.
World Bank data shows South Africa population: 2022=62.38M, 2023=63.21M. Both within [55, 68] range.
World Bank data shows South Africa electricity access: 2022=86.5%, 2023=87.7%. Both within [80, 100] range.

privileged_info_summary: Task requires research from the World Bank Open Data portal (data.worldbank.org) to collect five economic indicators (GDP, GDP per capita, life expectancy, population, electricity access) for Nigeria, Kenya, and South Africa. All plausibility ranges in metadata are accurate and verified against actual World Bank 2022-2023 data: Nigeria GDP $477.4B (2022), GDP per capita $2,139 (2022), life expectancy 54.1 years (2022), population 223.2M (2022), electricity access 60.5% (2022); Kenya GDP $114.5B (2022), GDP per capita $2,110 (2022), life expectancy 63.6 years (2022), population 54.3M (2022), electricity access 76% (2022); South Africa GDP $406.9B (2022), GDP per capita $6,523 (2022), life expectancy 65.5 years (2022), population 62.4M (2022), electricity access 86.5% (2022). All values fall within specified ranges. Data originates from the real, public World Bank Open Data portal, not synthetic data.
